clean_output:
summary: Retrieval is the clearest visible tension in this article: more BM25 context hurt performance, while oracle retrieval improved it, so access to code alone did not translate into better issue resolution.
review_confidence: high
contradictions:
  - contradiction_text: More retrieved code context did not improve resolution under BM25; performance fell as context grew, even though a simple expectation would be that giving the model more relevant repository material should help.
    related_claim_texts:
      - The paper’s retrieval results suggest that solution quality on SWE-bench is sensitive to context selection, because Claude 2 performed worse with larger BM25 contexts but better with oracle retrieval than with BM25 retrieval.
      - Under the paper’s reported evaluations, the best-performing model, Claude 2, resolved only 1.96% of SWE-bench issues.
    related_fact_refs:
      - fact-015
      - fact-016
      - fact-004
    related_evidence_ids:
      - evidence-15
      - evidence-16
      - evidence-04
    disposition: unresolved
    disclosure: Keep visible that "more context helps" is not supported here; the evidence supports a narrower conclusion that context selection quality matters, and that larger imperfectly retrieved contexts can degrade performance even while oracle retrieval improves it.
